descriptionInUI: Runtime for faked area mining. DCS has no mine object, so this scripts it: when a blue aircraft air-drops the designated cluster dispenser (CBU-99, carried only by the 'Retribution Minefield' loadout), the impact area becomes a proximity minefield. Any enemy ground unit -- a supply convoy -- that drives inside the field's radius trips a mine (one explosion at the vehicle, one charge spent); each unit trips at most once, and a field clears when its charges are exhausted. Fields are marked on the F10 map for your side only -- the enemy never sees them. Mines work the SAME mission they are laid: mine the road just ahead of an inbound convoy to stop it now. No phantom spawns: a mine kills a real, tracked convoy unit, so the loss is recorded natively at debrief (dead convoy units never arrive) -- this script owns no kills beyond producing the explosion. Blue-only: red does not lay mines and blue ground never trips them. (Cross-turn persistence and the auto-planned mining strike are added in later phases; this build is the same-turn tactical core.) All distances are metric.

specificOptions.nameInUI: Minefield radius from the drop point (m)
Mine density: chance a crossing vehicle trips a mine (%)
Minimum seconds between two detonations in one field
Startup grace before a field can detonate (s)
Dispenser: weapon-name matches that lay a field (comma-separated)
